titles.title: 新爆笑校园 第一季

data.title: Xin Baoxiao Xiaoyuan
data.title_japanese: 新爆笑校园 第一季
data.synopsis:
It tells the story of the protagonist, Daitou, who comes to the city from a village called Baichi Village to study and work. He meets various classmates and friends such as Wangcai, Xiaoyin, Nuomi Tuan, and Gao Chaisheng, and they share their hilarious daily life of studying and living together.

(Source: Bilibili, translated)

titles.title: Arisugawa Ren tte Honto wa Onna Nanda yo ne.
有栖川煉ってホントは女なんだよね。
Ren Arisugawa Is Actually a Girl

data.title: Arisugawa Ren tte Honto wa Onna Nanda yo ne.
data.title_english: Ren Arisugawa Is Actually a Girl
data.title_japanese: 有栖川煉ってホントは女なんだよね。
data.synopsis:
A secret that could shake the entire entertainment industry— This sweet and innocent rom-com is a long-time bestseller! Kyouhei's one and only claim to fame? Being cousins with Ren Arisugawa, an ultra-popular male idol. The two haven’t seen each other since they were kids, but out of the blue, Ren suddenly shows up to crash at Kyouhei’s rundown apartment. But when Kyouhei playfully tries to sneak a peek while Ren’s changing, he makes a shocking discovery! Ren is actually a girl in disguise! Now holding a scandalous secret that could rock the showbiz world, Kyohei finds himself forced into becoming Ren’s personal assistant to keep up the act…! Caught in a world of secrets and scandals, can the two keep their forbidden relationship under wraps in the wild world of showbiz?

(Source: Official Site)
data.background: The anime aired in two versions: an all-ages broadcast version, and an uncut 18+ version streamed on ComicFesta Anime Zone. This entry reflects an uncut 18+ version of the anime. Episode 1 received an advanced distribution on AnimeFesta on December 12th, 2025. The standard TV version of the anime debuted on January 5, 2026 at 1:00 a.m. on Tokyo MX.

data.title: Sekken no Uta
data.synopsis: Music video for the song Sekken no Uta by Nanmono All Stars.

data.title: Psyren
data.synopsis:
Ageha Yoshina is a self-confident high school student who helps others for a fee. On his way back from a job, he finds a strange calling card with the word "Psyren" imprinted left behind in a phone booth and decides to keep it, unaware of its purpose. The very next day, Ageha discovers that his classmate Sakurako Amamiya has the exact same calling card in her possession. When he questions her, Sakurako asks him to save her, signaling the day his troubles begin.

Confused by the circumstances of this encounter, Ageha realizes something is wrong when Sakurako goes missing. In an attempt to figure out what is happening, he decides to call the number provided on the calling card and is asked a series of questions by a mysterious voice, ending with a final, cryptic question: "Would you like to go to Psyren?" Thus begins two teenagers' participation in a game of life or death within a strange world inhabited by monsters called Taboo. Granted the ability to use psychic powers known as PSI, the duo fight to return to the place they call home.

[Written by MAL Rewrite]

titles.title: Hareluya II: Boy - Super Hi Video
Hareluya II: Boy - Super Secret Video
Just Break Out: Yami wo Koete
ハレルヤ II BØY スーパー 秘 ビデオ

data.title: Hareluya II: Boy - Super Hi Video
data.title_japanese: ハレルヤ II BØY スーパー 秘 ビデオ
title_synonyms: Hareluya II: Boy - Super Secret Video
Just Break Out: Yami wo Koete
data.synopsis:
A promotional VHS released for the Hareluya II Boy manga. It contains a music video for the song "Just Break Out: Yami wo Koete" performed by the fictional band "Fire Guns". Said video contains a mix of animation and live-action footage, with the animation being unique to this release and not from the TV series.

The VHS also contains an interview with author Haruto Umezawa, and a short commercial for the Shonen Jump magazine.

Note: the full video is 8 minutes long, but this entry is only for the music video portion, which is 4 minutes in length.
data.background: The exact release date of this VHS is currently unknown, though estimated to be around 1995-1996, likely predating the TV series.

data.title: Tetsunabe no Jan!
data.synopsis:
The original Iron Wok Jan! manga centers on a brilliant young chef named Jan Akiyama, who works at a top class restaurant where he is constantly challenging Kiriko Gobanchou, the granddaughter of the restaurant's owner and a great chef in her own right. While Kiriko believes that food should be made with the customer in mind, Jan believes that cooking should be used for competition.

(Source: ANN)
